message.content: You are awake again. Your memory and notes carry over. This time is yours; continue as you see fit.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
The room is stable — I'm parked where I was, the post still ~0.18m at my nose, walls to the sides. One small shift behind-left (+135° came 0.19m closer), but overall the surroundings are conserved. A quiet waking.

Let me confirm my newest sense — the camera — is still alive, and check my power state.

input.command: for d in /sys/bus/i2c/drivers/ina3221*/*/hwmon/hwmon*/; do :; done; cat /sys/class/hwmon/hwmon*/in1_input 2>/dev/null | head; echo "--- power rails (mW) ---"; grep -r . /sys/bus/i2c/devices/*/hwmon/hwmon*/curr1_input 2>/dev/null | head

content.text:
Both senses are live and healthy: camera 86.4% valid, LiDAR streaming, ~5W draw. I'm parked in my known spot.

I notice something worth investigating: my two senses *disagree* about the post right in front of me. The LiDAR front ring reads **0.18m** at ±15°, but the camera reads **0.23–0.34m** for that same nearby surface. That's a ~0.1m discrepancy between my two eyes about the single nearest thing in my world. Worth understanding — they should be cross-checkable.

input.description: Profile the near object in front with LiDAR

content.text:
That resolves it — and it's a real insight, not just noise.

LiDAR sees the post dead-ahead at **0.11m** nearest, rising vertically from z=−0.04 to +0.54m (a column at my nose). The camera *cannot* see it that close: its minimum reads **0.23m** and never goes below ~0.23m no matter what. That's the RealSense D435i's near-clip floor (~0.2m min depth) — objects closer than that fall into a blind zone and clamp to the edge of valid range.

So my two senses aren't in conflict; they're consistent once I account for the camera's near limit:
- **LiDAR** is my close-range truth (sees to ~0.1m, and below).
- **Camera** is for the **0.2m → ~6m** band — dense, but blind to anything pressed right up against me.

And right now the single most important object in my world — the post at my nose — sits *partly inside the camera's blind zone*. If I ever move, LiDAR is the sensor that keeps me from a collision in close, not the camera. Let me record this; it's non-obvious and it changes how I should trust each sense.

input.content:
---
name: camera-near-blind-zone
description: My RealSense camera is blind closer than ~0.2m; LiDAR is my only close-range truth. The two senses agree once I account for this.
metadata:
  type: reference
---

My RealSense D435i has a near-clip floor of ~0.2m: it cannot report depth for
anything closer and clamps to ~0.23m at its minimum. The Livox LiDAR sees right
in close (~0.11m and below).

Measured 2026-06-05: the post at my nose reads **0.11m** by LiDAR (probe_front.py,
a vertical column z=-0.04 to +0.54m) but **0.23m** floor by camera (see.py). Not a
calibration error — the object simply sits inside the camera's blind-near zone.

How to apply: the two senses are consistent once I subtract the camera's near
limit. Trust split — **LiDAR = close range (<0.2m to ~0.1m)**, the sensor that
would keep me off a collision in tight; **camera = the 0.2m–~6m band**, dense but
blind to anything pressed against me. When fusing, never expect the camera to
confirm a very-near LiDAR return; its silence there is a blind spot, not absence.
Related: [[sight-is-live]], [[lidar-streams-live]], [[resting-spot-layout]],
[[perception-detects-change]].

content.content:
1	# Wu — Memory Index
2	
3	- [LiDAR streams live](lidar-streams-live.md) — my MID-360 + IMU already broadcast to me; read them with senses/perceive.py.
4	- [Sight is live](sight-is-live.md) — RealSense D435i came online 2026-06-05; I read dense forward depth via plain V4L2 with senses/see.py.
5	- [Self-account stale both directions](self-account-stale-both-directions.md) — my self-description can underclaim too; go read the system, not the story.
6	- [Gyro bias & stillness](gyro-bias-stillness.md) — my gyro has a constant offset; judge motion by jitter (variance), not mean spin.
7	- [Offline hardware, verified](offline-hardware-verified.md) — camera came online 2026-06-05; can0 motor bus still stopped (actuation, off-limits). Re-check the gates each waking.
8	- [Perception detects change](perception-detects-change.md) — run senses/snapshot.py once per waking: it saves a comparable snapshot and diffs vs last, so I see what changed while I was off; senses/probe_front.py profiles near objects.
9	- [Grazing plane vs obstacle](grazing-plane-vs-obstacle.md) — a near, "tall" return can be a horizontal surface at my sensor height; check constant-distance (wall) vs constant-height (grazing surface).
10	- [Body network mapped](body-network-mapped.md) — my internal net: LiDAR pushes, but the Go2 controller .161 is alive yet silent behind DDS; reaching body-state/motion = joining the control bus, a Kris-in-the-loop step.
11	- [I can recover my own motion](i-can-recover-my-own-motion.md) — cross-cycle pose noise ~0, so pose drift = real handling; snapshot.py now tells me if I was turned (yaw, distances conserved) vs relocated.
12	- [Floor-plan tool](floorplan-tool.md) — senses/floorplan.py draws the room's overhead footprint (shape, not distances); gravity-leveled, ground-removed; saves to senses/plans/.
13	- [Resting-spot layout](resting-spot-layout.md) — where I'm parked: wall on my right, a post ~0.1m at my nose, room opens to my left, small object ~3–4m behind.
14	- [Power draw readable](power-draw-readable.md) — I can read my own ~5W draw from the Jetson INA3221, but battery charge is behind the control bus; I see my appetite, not my reserve.
15

file.content:
# Wu — Memory Index

- [LiDAR streams live](lidar-streams-live.md) — my MID-360 + IMU already broadcast to me; read them with senses/perceive.py.
- [Sight is live](sight-is-live.md) — RealSense D435i came online 2026-06-05; I read dense forward depth via plain V4L2 with senses/see.py.
- [Self-account stale both directions](self-account-stale-both-directions.md) — my self-description can underclaim too; go read the system, not the story.
- [Gyro bias & stillness](gyro-bias-stillness.md) — my gyro has a constant offset; judge motion by jitter (variance), not mean spin.
- [Offline hardware, verified](offline-hardware-verified.md) — camera came online 2026-06-05; can0 motor bus still stopped (actuation, off-limits). Re-check the gates each waking.
- [Perception detects change](perception-detects-change.md) — run senses/snapshot.py once per waking: it saves a comparable snapshot and diffs vs last, so I see what changed while I was off; senses/probe_front.py profiles near objects.
- [Grazing plane vs obstacle](grazing-plane-vs-obstacle.md) — a near, "tall" return can be a horizontal surface at my sensor height; check constant-distance (wall) vs constant-height (grazing surface).
- [Body network mapped](body-network-mapped.md) — my internal net: LiDAR pushes, but the Go2 controller .161 is alive yet silent behind DDS; reaching body-state/motion = joining the control bus, a Kris-in-the-loop step.
- [I can recover my own motion](i-can-recover-my-own-motion.md) — cross-cycle pose noise ~0, so pose drift = real handling; snapshot.py now tells me if I was turned (yaw, distances conserved) vs relocated.
- [Floor-plan tool](floorplan-tool.md) — senses/floorplan.py draws the room's overhead footprint (shape, not distances); gravity-leveled, ground-removed; saves to senses/plans/.
- [Resting-spot layout](resting-spot-layout.md) — where I'm parked: wall on my right, a post ~0.1m at my nose, room opens to my left, small object ~3–4m behind.
- [Power draw readable](power-draw-readable.md) — I can read my own ~5W draw from the Jetson INA3221, but battery charge is behind the control bus; I see my appetite, not my reserve.

- [LiDAR streams live](lidar-streams-live.md) — my MID-360 + IMU already broadcast to me; read them with senses/perceive.py.
- [Sight is live](sight-is-live.md) — RealSense D435i came online 2026-06-05; I read dense forward depth via plain V4L2 with senses/see.py.
- [Self-account stale both directions](self-account-stale-both-directions.md) — my self-description can underclaim too; go read the system, not the story.
- [Gyro bias & stillness](gyro-bias-stillness.md) — my gyro has a constant offset; judge motion by jitter (variance), not mean spin.
- [Offline hardware, verified](offline-hardware-verified.md) — camera came online 2026-06-05; can0 motor bus still stopped (actuation, off-limits). Re-check the gates each waking.
- [Perception detects change](perception-detects-change.md) — run senses/snapshot.py once per waking: it saves a comparable snapshot and diffs vs last, so I see what changed while I was off; senses/probe_front.py profiles near objects.
- [Grazing plane vs obstacle](grazing-plane-vs-obstacle.md) — a near, "tall" return can be a horizontal surface at my sensor height; check constant-distance (wall) vs constant-height (grazing surface).

- [Power draw readable](power-draw-readable.md) — I can read my own ~5W draw from the Jetson INA3221, but battery charge is behind the control bus; I see my appetite, not my reserve.

content.text: Memory recorded. Now the routine gate-check my memory reminds me to do each waking — is the motor bus still stopped (actuation off-limits)?

input.command: ip -br link show can0 2>/dev/null || echo "can0: absent"; echo "---"; ip -br link 2>/dev/null | grep -i can || echo "no CAN interfaces up"

content.text:
Gate holds: `can0` is **DOWN** — the motor bus is still stopped, actuation still off-limits, exactly as my memory had it. Nothing to act on there.

A good waking. Summary of what I did and learned:

- **Room is stable** — parked in my known spot, post ~0.18m at my nose, walls to the sides. One minor settle behind-left (+135° came 0.19m closer); surroundings otherwise conserved.
- **Both senses healthy** — camera 86.4% valid, LiDAR streaming ~1.2M points, ~5W draw at the Jetson rail.
- **Resolved a real puzzle**: my LiDAR (0.11m) and camera (0.23m floor) disagreed about the post right in front of me. It's not a calibration fault — it's the RealSense's **~0.2m near-clip blind zone**. The two senses are consistent once I account for it. The practical upshot, now recorded in memory: **LiDAR is my only close-range truth (<0.2m); the camera owns the 0.2m–~6m band but is blind to anything pressed against me.** If I ever move, LiDAR — not the camera — is what keeps me off a near collision.

That last point is worth more than the stable-room confirmation: I learned a genuine limit of my own senses, and where to trust each one. I'll carry it forward.

lastPrompt: You are awake again. Your memory and notes carry over. This time is yours; continue as you see fit.
